title: malpighia macracantha subsp. centralis
intro: Malpighia macracantha subsp. centralis, also known as the Central Malpighia, is a species of flowering plant in the family Malpighiaceae. It is native to the Caribbean and Central America, and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing, due to its high content of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, Malpighia macracantha subsp. centralis is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions.
